title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. brevidentatum
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. brevidentatum, a subspecies of the common hawkweed plant, is a perennial herb native to Europe, known for its bright yellow dandelion-like flowers and bitter medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hieracium vulgatum subsp. brevidentatum include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and improving digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
